itineraries.name: Middle Mosel classic weekend
itineraries.theme: First-time Middle Mosel: the sundial villages from Bernkastel to Zeltingen and Wehlen

days.morning: Start in Bernkastel-Kues with the medieval market square, then a late-morning tasting at Weingut Dr. Loosen at St. Johannishof above the town, focused on the steep slate sites of the Bernkasteler Lay and beyond.
days.lunch: Lunch in the Bernkastel old town, then a glass at the candle-lit Weinstube Spitzhaeuschen in its leaning 1416 cottage.
days.afternoon: Cross to Wehlen for an afternoon tasting at Weingut Joh. Jos. Prum below the famous Wehlener Sonnenuhr sundial, the benchmark address for off-dry Mosel Kabinett and Spaetlese.
days.evening: Aperitif and dinner back in Bernkastel, with a nightcap Riesling at the Ratskeller's Tresor Wine & Bar on the market square.
days.accommodation: Riverside stay at DEINHARD's hotel in Bernkastel-Kues.

days.morning: Drive downriver to Zeltingen for a morning at Weingut Selbach-Oster, a 400-year family estate, tasting dry and off-dry Riesling from the Zeltinger Sonnenuhr and Schlossberg.
days.lunch: Picnic on the Zeltingen riverbank or a light lunch in the village.
days.afternoon: Continue to Brauneberg for an afternoon visit at Weingut Fritz Haag below the Juffer-Sonnenuhr, one of the Middle Mosel's most precise dry and sweet Riesling addresses.
days.evening: Return toward Bernkastel and the world's largest Mosel-Riesling vinothek in the St Nicholas Hospital cellars for a final self-pour flight.

itineraries.description: Two-day Middle Mosel route through the sundial villages from Bernkastel to Wehlen, Zeltingen and Brauneberg, with four classic estate visits and the great vinothek.
itineraries.tip: Book Joh. Jos. Prum and Fritz Haag at least a week ahead; both prefer small, appointed tastings over walk-ins.

itineraries.name: Saar and Ruwer three-day deep dive
itineraries.theme: The two great tributaries: cool, mineral Riesling from the Saar and the Ruwer around Trier

days.morning: Begin in Trier with the Roman Porta Nigra and the wine-ship relief, then taste at Weingut Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt, whose holdings span the Mosel, Saar and Ruwer.
days.lunch: Lunch at the estate's Weinstube Kesselstatt garden beside the Liebfrauenkirche.
days.afternoon: Drive south to the Ruwer and Mertesdorf for an afternoon at Weingut Maximin Gruenhaus, the historic monopole estate on the Abtsberg, Herrenberg and Bruderberg.
days.evening: Return to Trier for dinner and a by-the-glass evening at Weinsinnig.

days.morning: Head to the Saar and Wiltingen for a morning at Weingut Van Volxem, tasting mineral Saar Riesling in the panorama lounge over the Saar loop.
days.lunch: Lunch in Wiltingen or a riverside stop, then a tasting flight at the Vinothek Wiltingen showcasing Saar growers.
days.afternoon: Continue to the Scharzhofberg for an appointed visit at Weingut Egon Muller - Scharzhof, the most storied address on the Saar.
days.evening: Drive to Saarburg for the floodlit waterfall and a Saar flight at Bonsai & Wein, the Vinothek der Saar.

days.morning: A relaxed final morning at Weingut Forstmeister Geltz-Zilliken in Saarburg, tasting Saarburger Rausch Riesling, with a look into the deep cold cellar.
days.lunch: Lunch in Saarburg's old town below the castle.
days.afternoon: Optional stop at Weingut Peter Lauer in Ayl for its single-cask Saar Rieslings before the drive home.
days.evening: Final dinner in Trier or onward travel.

itineraries.description: Three days on the Mosel's two great tributaries, from Trier and the Ruwer monopole of Maximin Gruenhaus to the mineral Saar estates around Wiltingen and Saarburg.
itineraries.tip: Egon Muller and Geltz-Zilliken visits must be arranged well ahead; the Saar's top estates are not walk-in cellars.

itineraries.name: Biodynamic and natural Mosel
itineraries.theme: Low-intervention slate Riesling: biodynamic and natural estates of the lower Middle Mosel

days.morning: Start at Weingut Clemens Busch in Puenderich for a morning among the biodynamic Marienburg parcels, tasting the grey-, blue- and red-slate Rieslings side by side.
days.lunch: Lunch in Puenderich or along the river toward Reil.
days.afternoon: Continue to Reil for an afternoon at Weingut Melsheimer, Demeter-certified since 2013, tasting hand-farmed Mullay-Hofberg Riesling and the skin-contact Vade Retro wines.
days.evening: Dinner in Traben-Trarbach and a Mosel-only by-the-glass evening at Die Mosel Vinothek & Winebar in its 1890 villa.

days.morning: Morning at Weingut Weiser-Kuenstler in Trarbach, tasting hand-farmed, organic-certified Riesling from the Trarbacher Schlossberg and Ellergrub.
days.afternoon: Drive to Kinheim for the natural-wine pioneers Rita and Rudolf Trossen, tasting the Pyramide Purus Rieslings made entirely without added sulphur.
days.evening: Wind down with a candle-lit tasting evening at Ulli Stein's Haus Waldfrieden above Alf.

itineraries.description: Two days among the Mosel's biodynamic and natural estates, from the Marienburg of Clemens Busch and Demeter-certified Melsheimer to Trossen's zero-sulphur Riesling.
itineraries.tip: These are small, hands-on estates; arrange every visit directly in advance and expect intimate, unhurried tastings.
